Title: The Fie1-PRC2 complex regulates H3K27me3 deposition to balance endosperm filling and development in cereals

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2) core component FIE; WD40-repeat Polycomb group protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Balances endosperm filling and development; represses excessive α-zein accumulation and endosperm cell proliferation; loss causes smaller kernels with increased α-zein and earlier cellularization
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage/nitrogen reservoir protein for grain filling and seed nutrition
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Maize endosperm protein bodies, especially starchy endosperm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endosperm-specific; high from En72HAP and during 6–10 DAP around onset of filling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 10-DAP endosperm; highly expressed from En3D to En10D
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; EMS mutant; H3K27me3 profiling; RNA-seq; protein assays; cytology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmFie1 is an endosperm-specific PRC2 component highly expressed at the onset of grain filling. 2. Loss of ZmFie1 reduces H3K27me3 at filling-specific peaks, especially α-zein and ZmMADS loci. 3. zmfie1 mutants show premature cellularization, enhanced endosperm cell proliferation, smaller kernels, increased 19- and 22-kDa α-zeins, larger protein bodies, and slightly reduced starch.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Endosperm developmental stage around grain filling (2–20 DAP), especially 6–12 DAP
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts through a grain-specific FIE1-PRC2 complex; effects can be partially confounded by compensatory upregulation of ZmFie2 and gained H3K27me3 peaks in mutant.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced kernel size and weight; reduced opaque endosperm area; slight reduction in starch granule diameter/total starch in some backgrounds
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful epigenetic target to tune cereal endosperm protein accumulation versus grain size/development; deployment should consider tradeoff between higher zein/protein and smaller kernels, and likely requires balancing with starch/yield traits or precise temporal modulation.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: ZmFie1 itself functions as an epigenetic regulator mediating H3K27me3 deposition at filling-specific peaks; discussion suggests some target loci may later convert from H3K27me3 to more stable DNA methylation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Polycomb repressive complex 2 (PRC2) core component FIE; WD40-repeat Polycomb group protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses 13-kDa prolamin gene expression and endosperm cell proliferation by H3K27me3 deposition; loss causes increased prolamin transcript accumulation and enhanced early cell proliferation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage/nitrogen reservoir protein in rice endosperm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to ZmFie1 according to paper; grain/endosperm-associated during early filling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFie1 loss causes reduction of H3K27me3 at 13-kDa prolamin and OsMADS loci. 2. 13-kDa prolamin genes in RM4 cluster and LOC_Os07g10580 are strongly upregulated in osfie1. 3. osfie1 shows enhanced early endosperm cell proliferation, indicating conserved FIE1-PRC2 function across cereals.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite higher 13-kDa prolamin transcript levels, mature grain total storage protein and 13-kDa prolamin content were not obviously changed in rice.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: CUT&Tag; RT-qPCR; dataset within BioProject PRJNA1172298
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter endosperm developmental balance; no obvious increase in mature storage protein observed
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential epigenetic lever for modulating cereal storage-protein gene expression and endosperm development, but mature protein outcome in rice may be buffered; engineering should consider species-specific disconnect between transcript and final protein content.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: OsFie1 is an epigenetic regulator mediating H3K27me3 deposition at storage-protein and OsMADS loci.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40-repeat Polycomb group protein; PRC2 core subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Chromatin regulator / Polycomb repressive complex 2 component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: H3K27me3 deposition leading to transcriptional repression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage-protein gene repression and developmental balance during endosperm filling; indirect control of cell proliferation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: α-zein genes, especially 19-kDa z1A and 22-kDa α-zein tandem repeats on chromosome 4; ZmMADS loci
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses α-zein and selected ZmMADS genes via H3K27me3 deposition at filling-specific peaks
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Limits premature/overactive α-zein expression and suppresses excessive endosperm cell proliferation, balancing filling and development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Grain-specific major epigenetic controller that establishes filling-specific H3K27me3 peaks at storage-protein loci during onset of endosperm filling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endosperm-specific, high from En72HAP and 6–10 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage of endosperm filling; cereal grain-specific expression program
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not established; mutant shows compensatory upregulation of ZmFie2/MEZ1/Fie2-related methyltransferase loci
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Yes; ZmFie1-PRC2 complex with ZmEmf2b, ZmMSI1c, and ZmMEZ1 inferred from expression and prior interaction data
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; EMS mutant; H3K27me3 CUT&Tag and ChIP-seq; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; protein assays; cytology
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmFie1 mediates a large portion of filling-specific H3K27me3 peaks in maize endosperm. 2. Loss of ZmFie1 derepresses α-zein genes and alters ZmMADS regulation. 3. ZmFie1 suppresses premature cellularization and later endosperm cell proliferation, coupling development with storage-protein deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased zein and slightly increased total protein but smaller kernels and lower kernel weight; possible starch reduction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for epigenetic breeding to modify storage-protein synthesis and grain filling, but requires careful balancing with kernel size/yield and likely temporal or partial modulation rather than complete knockout.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: H3K27me3 loss is necessary but not always sufficient for target activation; some MADS loci may require additional epigenetic changes.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct mediator of H3K27me3 deposition; discussion proposes possible transition of some targets to DNA methylation after repression.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40-repeat Polycomb group protein; PRC2 core subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Chromatin regulator / Polycomb repressive complex 2 component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: H3K27me3 deposition leading to transcriptional repression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage-protein gene repression and endosperm cell proliferation control
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 13-kDa prolamin genes in RM4 cluster on chromosome 5; LOC_Os07g10580 in RM1 cluster; OsMADS loci including OsMADS8, OsMADS18, OsMADS58
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses 13-kDa prolamin genes and selected OsMADS genes via H3K27me3 deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses prolamin gene expression and excessive early endosperm cell proliferation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved cereal grain-specific epigenetic regulator controlling storage-protein gene chromatin state
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed in 5-DAP caryopses; developmental effects from 5–7 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Null mutant; CUT&Tag; RT-qPCR; semi-thin sectioning and cell counting
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFie1 loss reduces H3K27me3 at 13-kDa prolamin clusters and OsMADS loci. 2. Prolamin transcripts increase strongly in osfie1. 3. osfie1 displays increased endosperm cell numbers at 6 DAP, supporting conserved FIE1-PRC2 function in cereals.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transcript increase does not necessarily translate to higher mature grain protein; developmental perturbation possible
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate epigenetic regulator for modulating storage-protein transcription in rice, though mature protein outcomes and species-specific buffering must be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conservation with maize is stronger at chromatin and transcript levels than at final mature storage-protein phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct mediator of H3K27me3 deposition at target loci.
